title: basella oleracea var. rubra
intro: Basella oleracea var. rubra, also known as red malabar spinach, is a variety of the Basella oleracea plant that belongs to the Basellaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as lowering blood sugar levels, reducing cholesterol and triglyceride levels, and exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, red malabar spinach is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and it can also be consumed raw in salads or cooked as a leafy green vegetable.
